architecture_plan.title: NEX-DSim Fast End-to-End Performance Simulation Architecture
architecture_plan.overview: An implementation architecture for fast end-to-end performance simulation of accelerated hardware-software stacks, based on the NEX orchestrator and DSim di-simulator concepts. The system adopts a minimalist approach by simulating only unavailable components while running the rest natively, with precise synchronization between native and simulated execution. The architecture supports modular composition and provides accurate, high-speed simulation for accelerated systems.

modules.purpose: Manages the overall simulation workflow, including synchronization, runtime execution, scheduling, and time warping for native and simulated components.
Handles the correct and efficient synchronization between natively executing components and simulated components to maintain system coherence.
Provides the runtime environment for executing native components and managing their interaction with simulated components.
Schedules execution of native and simulated components, optimizing for performance and ensuring correct temporal ordering.
Implements time warping mechanisms to align simulation time with real-time or accelerated execution time.
Simulates performance-critical aspects of unavailable hardware/software components using Linear Process Networks (LPNs) and maintains functionality consistency.
Computes performance metrics for simulated components using LPNs and integrates them with native execution.
Ensures functional correctness of simulated components by modeling their behavior accurately.
Coordinates synchronization between performance and functionality computations in the DSim component.
Provides modular interfaces for composing different simulation components and enabling interoperability between NEX and DSim.

framework_suggestions: SimPy for discrete event simulation and time management
NumPy for numerical computations and performance modeling
Pybind11 for C++ integration if needed for performance-critical native components
PyQt or Tkinter for visualization of simulation progress and results
pytest for unit testing of simulation modules
